title: What is Question Answering?
concept: Question Answering (QA) takes a context paragraph and a question, retrieving or generating the correct answer.

exercises.prompt: What does a Question Answering (QA) model do?
exercises.explain: Question Answering (QA) models are designed to find specific answers inside a text, helping us get information quickly.

cards.title: Instant Answers
cards.body: Have you ever asked a smart speaker a question and had it read back a perfect sentence from a webpage? That is the magic of Question Answering AI!

cards.title: What is Question Answering?
cards.body: Question Answering (QA) is an AI task where a computer reads a paragraph (called the context) and a question, then finds or generates the correct answer based on that paragraph.

cards.title: The Open-Book Test
cards.body: Imagine you have an open book and a sheet of questions. You don't know the answers by heart, but you can read the page (context) to find the exact sentence that answers each question. That's exactly how Question Answering works!

cards.title: QA in Action
cards.body:
Context: "Luna the cat loves sleeping on the sunny windowsill."
Question: "Where does Luna love to sleep?"
Answer: "the sunny windowsill"
The AI scans the text and pulls out the exact spot Luna likes.

cards.title: They Don't Know It All
cards.body:
Myth: Question Answering systems know everything about the world by heart.
Fact: Most QA systems need to be given a specific text (context) to read in order to find the correct answer!

cards.title: Search vs. Answer
cards.body: Unlike search engines that just give you web links, QA systems read the text for you and give you the direct answer.

left.text: Gives you 10 websites to read yourself to find the answer.

right.text: Reads the text and extracts the exact answer sentence for you.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of a Question Answering (QA) system?

options.text: To find or generate answers using a provided text
To translate text from English to French
To write a creative story from scratch
To check if words are spelled correctly

exercises.explain: Question Answering models extract or generate answers from a specific text you provide. They don't just chat or make up random stories.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A QA system needs both a question and a context paragraph to work correctly.
exercises.explain: A QA system uses the context paragraph as its source of truth to locate the answer to the question.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of QA inputs:
exercises.sentence: A QA model reads a question and a paragraph called the ________.
exercises.explain: The context is the background paragraph where the AI searches for the answer to the question.

exercises.prompt: Which of these shows a true Question Answering task?

options.text: Example A: "Hi! I am a robot. Ask me anything about history!"
Example B: Context: "Bananas grow on trees." Question: "Where do bananas grow?" Answer: "on trees"
Example C: "Type your query here to search the entire web."

exercises.explain: Example B shows the AI actually reading the context paragraph and pulling out the correct answer to the question. Example A is just a chat bot response.

exercises.prompt: To run a Question Answering system, what two inputs does the AI need?

options.text: Only a question, and it guesses based on memory
Only a paragraph, and it summarizes the entire thing
Both a context paragraph and a question to find the answer
A list of keywords to search on the web

exercises.explain: A Question Answering model takes both the context (the text containing the information) and the question to successfully find or write the correct answer.
